entry.title: Main Page
entry.content:
Zian Wang
School of Computing and Information, University of Pittsburgh
Contact : ziw42@pitt.edu

About Me

I am a graduate student studying at the University of Pittsburgh, School of Computing and Information. My major is Infomation Science. I will graduate in December 2022 and am applying for Ph.D. programs. I received two Bachelor’s degrees in Computer Science and Computing and Information Science during my undergraduate period.

I mainly do research in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Bayesian modeling fields. I think NLP is an important field that can make machines not only utilize quintillions of bytes of data generated by humans each day but also learn human past knowledge and experience, to solve real-world problems. I have made a project of sentiment analysis of Tweets posted during the COVID-19 pandemic, and a project of detecting unfair terms in the police union contracts. About Bayesian modeling, I learned a lot about it in my machine learning course, and I am currently working on a review project on Bayesian optimization under the guidance of Dr. Joseph Yurko.

I am currently doing research in the PICSO lab of Dr. Yuru Lin at our school. I myself have finished a paper on using Transfer Learning to apply BERT models to downstream tasks, and it has been delivered to be reviewed.

Also, I am interested in most of the general directions in machine learning, such as Human Computer Interaction (HCI), Trustworthy ML, Deep Learning, and Reinforce Learning. I am still a “curious” student in the ocean of machine learning, so I am definitely willing to explore and learn more about this “exciting” and “magic” ocean. Opportunities for possible Ph.D. positions in all of these fields are appreciated.

Here is my CV and my transcript

Papers

(Forgive me for calling this section “papers” rather than “publications” since the articles have not been published yet.)

2022:
A New Computationally Efficient Method to Tune BERT Networks – Transfer Learning
Zian Wang
Accepted by 2023 International Conference on Signal Processing and Machine Learning (CONF-SPML 2023).

2021:
Police Union Contract Misconduct Complaint Detection
Zian Wang, Sonal Gupta, Shuo Zheng
A final report paper for the Data Mining course, topic is given by Dr. Lin Yuru.
entry.summary:
Zian Wang
School of Computing and Information, University of Pittsburgh
